text: Consider bootstrap stub that delegates to tracker-branch tool

current vendoring model (clone to tools/gittoc/) is working well and is simple to understand. The bootstrap-stub architecture would reduce visible-branch churn but adds indirection and complexity. Only worth revisiting if the vendored-copy model causes real pain (e.g. updating gittoc across many repos becomes a burden).
Related bootstrap work: see T-81 for the user-facing single-command bootstrap flow. T-35 is narrower and more conceptual (stub/delegation), so triage together before implementation.

Design discussion summary: the branch model (tool lives on the gittoc branch alongside tracker state) is the preferred direction. Key points:

INSTALLATION (fresh project):
  git remote add gittoc https://codeberg.org/dpiegdon/gittoc
  git fetch gittoc
  git branch gittoc gittoc/main  # local branch = tool code as base
  git worktree add .git/gittoc gittoc
  printf '[alias]\n    toc = !.git/gittoc/gittoc\n' >> .git/config

UPDATES (clean, always conflict-free):
  cd .git/gittoc && git fetch gittoc && git merge gittoc/main
  This works because gittoc/main only touches tool files (gittoc_lib/, SKILL.md, ...)
  and local tracker commits only touch issues/ — paths never overlap.

FRESH CLONE SETUP (3 manual lines, unavoidable):
  git remote add gittoc https://codeberg.org/dpiegdon/gittoc
  git fetch gittoc
  .git/gittoc/setup  (setup script lives on the gittoc branch itself)
  Chicken-and-egg: worktree must exist before setup script is accessible.

ZERO-POLLUTION MODE (valid use case):
  Skip the skill file and .gittoc config entirely.
  Accept that fresh-clone setup is manual and agents need explicit pointing.
  The worktree (.git/gittoc/) and alias (.git/config) are never committed.
  Zero upstream impact.

AGENT AUTO-DISCOVERY (optional, minimal pollution):
  Commit .claude/skills/gittoc.md (real file copy of SKILL.md, ~2KB) to main.
  Claude auto-discovers it. Always readable even if worktree not set up yet.
  Optional .gittoc config file with remote URL enables scripted fresh-clone setup.

INIT CHANGES NEEDED:
  Current _bootstrap_worktree creates an orphan gittoc branch with just issues/.
  New model: gittoc branch already exists (based on gittoc/main with tool code),
  init only needs to create issues/ directory structure and first commit.
  Orphan-branch logic goes away.
